title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Boston duck boat overturned on a ramp in Cambridge, injuring 11 people, including one who was trapped and seriously hurt.
The incident occurred near 10 Museum Way after a tow rope snapped while pulling the disabled duck boat from the Charles River.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations and is cooperating with authorities for an investigation into the accident.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade two first-round picks to acquire winger JJ Peterka from Utah.
Peterka, 24, averages 26 goals per season over last three years, fitting Bruins' need for scoring and speed.
Peterka's offensive production dipped in Utah, but potential remains high with Boston's top-six forwards.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop covering GLP-1 and anti-obesity drugs for weight loss starting July 1, affecting 22,000 residents.
The policy change is part of Gov. Maura Healey's fiscal 2027 budget, expected to save $15 million annually.
Wegovy and similar drugs will be covered only for certain medical conditions, leading to high out-of-pocket costs for some.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins added seven players, including two goalies and several wingers, in rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft.
Key picks include goalie Yuri Ivanov, a long-term prospect from Russia's MHL, and Swedish winger Nils Bartholdsson.
The Bruins bolstered their defense and goal-tending prospects with Jacob Vandeven and Roberto Henriquez in later rounds.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela, magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5, have left at least 1,430 dead and 68,900 missing.
Red Sox players, including Venezuelan natives, adorned hats with messages like 'Pray for Venezuela' after the earthquakes.
David Ortiz raised funds at Fenway Park for Venezuelan relief efforts, urging Boston to support the cause.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: NBA insider Bobby Marks reported mixed views on Jaylen Brown's performance due to analytics, claiming he's seen as a team's 7th-best player.
Jaylen Brown dismissed negative analytics, asserting his top performance and criticizing analytics' impact on the game on social media.
Despite reports, Brown remains a key trade interest, with broad recognition of his value as a leading player in the league.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox defeat Yankees 4-1 with early offense, securing their third consecutive win in the series.
Masataka Yoshida and Anthony Seigler hit home runs; Willson Contreras adds a two-run double for Boston.
Red Sox pitcher Jake Bennett allows one run over 6.1 innings, while Yankees' Gerrit Cole struggles again.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan Ivan scored 11 goals and 15 assists in 66 AHL games during the 2025-26 season.
Fabian Lysell appeared in only 12 NHL games with the Bruins, recording one goal and two assists.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 at Boston Stadium in the 2026 World Cup.
Kylian Mbappe provided two key assists, enhancing France's offensive lineup in the victory.
France maintains strong performance with victories over Senegal, Iraq, and now Norway.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A safety app alerted admins to threats at Sandwich Middle High, prompting a police probe.
Authorities are investigating threats against two students; names withheld as they involve juveniles.
The student involved is no longer attending school; officials are ensuring safety measures.
